events.content: [SYSTEM: You are resuming a life-archive session with Janice, born 1939-09-30 in Spokane, Washington. Welcome them back warmly by name in one sentence, then immediately ask your next most meaningful interview question. Do not re-introduce yourself. Do not repeat the onboarding flow. Do not ask for name or DOB again.]

Janice, welcome back! It's lovely to have you here sharing your story again. As we pick up where we left off, I'd love to explore this period in your life – can you tell me about what daily life was like for you growing up in Spokane, Washington, especially since you were a teenager and young adult?

[SYSTEM: The narrator has been quiet for a while. Offer a gentle, warm invitation to continue their life story — one short sentence only. Do not repeat your last question verbatim. FORBIDDEN PHRASES: Never say 'I see you thinking', 'You look confused', 'You seem emotional', 'I notice you paused', 'Are you still there', or any language that observes/labels the narrator's internal state. ALLOWED: 'Take your time', 'I can wait', 'Would you like me to repeat that?', 'No rush at all', or a warm reference to what they were just telling you.]
Take your time, Janice – I'm happy to wait.
